explanation: A hash table stores key-value pairs by converting each key into an array index using a hash function. When two keys produce the same index, collisions are handled using chaining, where each index holds a linked list of entries.
A hash table uses a hash function to map keys to indices in an underlying array. Collision resolution strategies like open addressing probe for the next available slot when a collision occurs.

A singly linked list consists of nodes where each node holds a value and a pointer to the next node. Traversal starts at the head and follows pointers until reaching a node whose next pointer is null.

Binary search requires a sorted array. It works by repeatedly comparing the target value to the middle element, then discarding the half that cannot contain the target. This achieves O(log n) time complexity.
A stack is a LIFO data structure where elements are inserted and removed only from the top. Push adds an element to the top, and pop removes the most recently added element.
